question: What is the default behaviour of `propagateAllChildVariables` on a call activity, and what is its effect on completion?

options.text: `true` — every variable of the created child process instance is propagated back to the call activity scope when the child completes.
`false` — only variables matching the call activity's output mappings are propagated; you must opt in to propagate everything.
`true` — only the child's local variables are propagated; variables in higher scopes of the child are excluded.
`false` — variables remain in the child and the parent must fetch them via a job worker.

a.text: The default for `propagateAllChildVariables` is `true`: all variables of the created process instance are propagated to the call activity when it completes.

b.text: Defining output mappings is the way to opt out of full propagation, but it is not the default. By default, every child variable is propagated.

c.text: The propagation rule covers all variables of the child instance, not just its local variables. Local-only scoping is configured differently and on the parent side.

d.text: Variables are propagated automatically when the call activity completes; no separate job-worker fetch is needed by default.

explanation: By default, `propagateAllChildVariables` is `true`, so all variables of the child process instance are merged back into the call activity's scope on completion. Defining output mappings on the call activity restricts propagation to only the mapped variables.
